en.extract:
The fan-tailed grassbird or broad-tailed warbler (Catriscus brevirostris) is an African species of Old World warbler in the family Locustellidae. The species is closely related to the broad-tailed grassbird of India, and is sometimes treated as the same species, although a 2018 study found that it and the broad-tailed grassbird were not closely related, with the Indian species being a sister of Chaetornis striata.
The species has a discontinuous distribution across Africa, and is found in Angola, Burundi, Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Eswatini, Equatorial Guinea, Ethiopia, Gabon, Guinea, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Nigeria, Rwanda, Sierra Leone, South Africa, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. It is found in grassy areas dominated by grasses, sedges or shrubs near water (streams, rivers or lakes), from 350–2,150 m (1,150–7,050 ft).

''Schoenicola brevirostris'' }} The '''fan-tailed grassbird''' or '''broad-tailed warbler''' ('''''Catriscus brevirostris''''') is an African species of [[Old World warbler]] in the family [[Locustellidae]]. The species is closely related to the [[broad-tailed grassbird]] of India, and is sometimes treated as the same species,{{Cite taxon|HBWa|author=Madge, S. |year=2017|title= Fan-tailed Grassbird (''Schoenicola brevirostris'')|url=http://www.hbw.com/node/58725|access-date= 17 March 2017}} although a 2018 study found that it and the broad-tailed grassbird were not closely related, with the Indian species being a sister of ''Chaetornis striata''.{{cite journal|doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2018.03.029|title=Comprehensive molecular phylogeny of the grassbirds and allies (Locustellidae) reveals extensive non-monophyly of traditional genera, and a proposal for a new classification|journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution|year=2018|last1=Alström|first1=Per|last2=Cibois|first2=Alice|last3=Irestedt|first3=Martin|last4=Zuccon|first4=Dario|last5=Gelang|first5=Magnus|last6=Fjeldså|first6=Jon|last7=Andersen|first7=Michael J|last8=Moyle|first8=Robert G|last9=Pasquet|first9=Eric|last10=Olsson|first10=Urban|volume=127|pages=367–375|pmid=29625229|s2cid=4645834 |url=https://zenodo.org/record/2587007}} The species has a discontinuous distribution across Africa, and is found in [[Angola]], [[Burundi]], [[Cameroon]], [[Republic of the Congo]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Eswatini]], [[Equatorial Guinea]], [[Ethiopia]], [[Gabon]], [[Guinea]], [[Kenya]], [[Malawi]], [[Mozambique]], [[Nigeria]], [[Rwanda]], [[Sierra Leone]], [[South Africa]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], [[Uganda]], [[Zambia]], and [[Zimbabwe]]. It is found in grassy areas dominated by grasses, sedges or shrubs near water (streams, rivers or lakes), from {{cvt|350|–|2150|m|ft|-1}}. ==Gallery== Ornithologie Nordost-Afrika's - der Nilquellen- und Küsten Gebiete des Rothen Meeres und des nördlichen Somal-Landes (1869) (14566057207).jpg Schoenicola brevirostris 1.jpg Schoenicola brevirostris Broad-tailed warbler 2014 11 05.jpg ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Catriscus brevirostris}} * Fan-tailed grassbird - [http://sabap2.adu.org.za/docs/sabap1/642.pdf Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds]. {{Passerida|S.|state=collapsed}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q62131914|from2=Q3726383}} [[Category:Locustellidae|fan-tailed grassbird]] [[Category:Birds of Sub-Saharan Africa]] [[Category:Birds described in 1850|fan-tailed grassbird]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] {{Locustellidae-stub}}
